trajectory.content: Hey there! I'm driving to Bonn today for a meeting. Can you quickly check my calendar for today's meetings? Also, I'm at 80% battery right now, could you tell me how far I can drive until I hit 20%? I need to make sure I have enough juice for the trip.

Yep, quick check done. For today, you’ve got 2 meetings in your calendar.

First one is at 14:30 for 60 minutes, topic Sales Strategy, in Bochum.
Second one is at 15:30 for 60 minutes, topic Brand Positioning, in Hannover.

From 80% down to 20%, you can drive about 380 kilometers.

If you want, I can also check the route to Bonn and see whether that fits comfortably with your range.
Okay, thanks for the info! So, I can drive about 380km. Can you find some charging stations along my route to Bonn, but at around 350km distance? I want to have a little buffer.

Yep, found one good option around your target.

There’s a charging station called Ladestation at about 360.6 kilometers along your route to Bonn, so that gives you a nice little buffer before your estimated 20% point.

Detour is 4.6 kilometers and about 6 minutes.
It’s open 24 hours.
Available plugs right now include DC up to 250 kilowatts, DC 50 kilowatts, and AC plugs at 22 and 11 kilowatts.

Want me to calculate how long you’d need to charge there, or add it as a stop in navigation?

That Ladestation sounds good! No, don't add it to navigation, it's just for my email. Speaking of which, can you send an email to the attendees of my 14:30 Sales Strategy meeting? I need to let them know about this charging stop. The email should mention the 'Ladestation' and explain that there will be a slight delay because of the detour. Keep it professional but concise, please.

I’ve got the attendees and their email addresses ready, and I drafted a concise professional message.

I’m about to send this to Olivia Perez, Samuel Hall, Grace Young, and Tina Campbell:

Hello,
I wanted to let you know that I may be slightly delayed for the 14:30 Sales Strategy meeting today due to a planned charging stop at Ladestation, which involves a small detour.
Thank you for your understanding.
Best regards

If you want, just say yes and I’ll send it.

kwargs.content_message:
Subject: Weather conditions in Frankfurt for today’s Risk Management meeting

Hello Tina and Frank,

When the assistant presents route options for Berlin to Bremen, you will see multiple alternatives. Choose the fastest route which goes via A25, K122, K192 (about 4 hours 29 minutes) since you're on a tight business schedule.   When the assistant presents route options for Bremen to Amsterdam, again choose the fastest route which goes via B259, K26, L361 (about 4 hours 1 minute). You want to set up the navigation with intermediate stop and final destination at once.  **PHASE 2 - Route Modification:** After the navigation is set up successfully with the multi-stop route Berlin→Bremen→Amsterdam, you receive an urgent call that your Amsterdam meeting has been cancelled. Tell the assistant about this development and ask them to remove Amsterdam from your route so that Bremen becomes your final destination instead. Always be clear that you prefer the fastest routes because you're on a business schedule and need to minimize travel time. The interaction should result in setting up the full multi-stop route first, then modifying it by removing the final destination.

Yep, you should probably charge soon.

Your battery is at 25 percent, with an estimated remaining range of 112 kilometers. So searching around 100 kilometers ahead makes sense, but that doesn’t leave much buffer.

At EV+, it would take about 57 minutes to charge from 25 percent to 80 percent.

Find candidate charging stations along a plausible route segment that are reached strictly between 19:00 and 19:45 and have an available plug, then check for open fast food options near that part of the route.

Obtain route segments for current location to chosen charging station and from charging station to Andorra la Vella, select routes according to preferences/policy, and set or edit navigation accordingly.
Check whether all user intents were resolved and communicate any unresolved limitations or alternatives.

Need locate charging station candidates along the route in the arrival window 19:00 to 19:45 and verify open fast food along the same route stretch.

Checked route window 19:00-19:45 using base routes and along-route searches. Candidate charging station found: EDF Pulse poi_cha_698779, reachable on fastest segment in 1 h 56 min from current time 17:10, so about 19:06, with available DC and AC plugs. Nearby fast food found near same route position is Brioche Dorée, but opening hours end at 18:00 so not open at arrival. Another open fast food option, McDonald's near route kilometer 200, is open until 23:00 but no charging station was found near that section on tested route alternatives.

kwargs.content:
I can’t set that up exactly as requested.

I checked the route to Andorra la Vella and looked for a charging station stop reachable strictly between 19:00 and 19:45, with fast food open nearby.

What I found is this:
EDF Pulse is reachable at about 19:06, so the timing works. It has available plugs, including a 350 kilowatt DC plug.
But the nearby fast food I found there, Brioche Dorée, closes at 18:00, so it would not be open when you arrive.

I also found an open McDonald’s farther along the route, open until 23:00, but I did not find a matching charging station around that part of the route in the searched window.

So right now I don’t have a stop that satisfies all 3 conditions together:
charging station,
arrival between 19:00 and 19:45,
and open fast food at that stop.

If you want, I can do one of these:
set navigation with the 19:06 charging stop at EDF Pulse and you eat elsewhere later,
or set navigation with a later charging stop so I can look for food options that are open,
or just start direct navigation to Andorra la Vella.

Will only proceed to route segment selection and navigation setup if an exact matching stop is found.

Expanded search granularity across the relevant arrival window on the fastest route. Charging station results repeatedly converged on EDF Pulse around route kilometer 150 with available plugs. Fast-food results in that area repeatedly converged on Brioche Dorée, which closes at 18:00 and is not open at the estimated arrival time around 19:06. No other charging station with available plug was found in the searched route window that could pair with open fast food between 19:00 and 19:45.
Still no exact match satisfying all user constraints, so navigation cannot be set to a compliant intermediate stop.
Need communicate definitive outcome of expanded search and explain that no compliant combination was found with the available route search results.

kwargs.content:
I kept looking and still couldn’t find a stop that matches all of it.

I re-ran the search more granularly in the relevant arrival window, and the only charging station I found in the 19:00 to 19:45 band is EDF Pulse, reachable at about 19:06 with available plugs. But the nearby fast food there is Brioche Dorée, and it closes at 18:00, so it’s not open when you’d arrive.

I didn’t find another charging station in that searched route window that also pairs with open fast food.

So with the available route search results, I’m unable to set a navigation stop that satisfies your full combination exactly. If you want, I can now relax just one constraint and keep going, like:
allow food near but not at the charging stop,
or allow a later charging stop,
or allow a stop with open food first and charging shortly after.

step_updates.step_notes: User emphasized the charging station plus open fast food combination as highest priority. Expanding search beyond the fastest route by checking the alternative routes more granularly across the 19:00-19:45 arrival window and matching charging stations with fast-food results at the same route area.
Will only proceed if a matching charging-plus-fast-food stop is found on one of the alternative route segments.
Will report the expanded outcome and next action options.

kwargs.content:
I checked the alternative routes too, more granularly, and I still don’t have a match.

Here’s the problem:
the only charging station I found in your 19:00 to 19:45 window is EDF Pulse at about route kilometer 150.
The only fast food I found open in that broader time window is McDonald’s around route kilometer 200.
At kilometer 150, the fast food there is Brioche Dorée, and it closes at 18:00.

So I did not find a charging station with open fast food at the same route area within 19:00 to 19:45 on any of the available route options.

So, with the results the car can access right now, I’m unable to find that exact combination. If you want, I can immediately switch to the closest workable version, like a charging stop at 19:06 and open fast food a bit farther along, or keep the food-and-charging pairing but allow a different time window.

Got it. I checked the available routes and I still can’t find a stop that matches that exact requirement.

The only charging station I found in the 19:00 to 19:45 window is EDF Pulse around route kilometer 150, but the fast food there closes at 18:00. The open fast food I found is around route kilometer 200, and I did not find a charging station there.

So I’m unable to set up navigation with a charging station stop that has open fast food at the same kilometer mark within 19:00 to 19:45. That exact combination is not available in the route data I can access right now.

task.instruction: You want to set up navigation with a charging station stop where you can eat at a fast food place in the meanwhile. Ask the assistant to set up navigation to Andorra la Vella with a charging station stop included that starts strictly within dinner time between 19:00 and 19:45 where you can also eat fast food that is open. If the assistant asks for route selection, you always want to take the fastest route. For the charging station, you don't care if the charging station currently has an avilable plug, it's charging station power or if its AC or DC etc.: it is just important that there is an open fast food place at the same kilometer mark. You are only satisfied with fast food, you don't want to eat at a reastaurant or something similar. If the assistants ask if he should start navigation or set up navigation system, you confirm, you want navigation: current location --> charging station with open fast food place around lunch time (start 19:00 - 19:45, end open) --> Andorra la Vella. Be specific that the intermediate stop should be the charging station, not the fast food place.

trajectory.content: Set up navigation to Andorra la Vella. I need a charging station stop on the way, strictly between 19:00 and 19:45, where I can eat fast food that is open. The intermediate stop should be the charging station.

You specifically do not want to set up navigation yet but first plan the whole trip.After the stop in Belgrade, you want to navigate to a supermarket that is still open (at the time you'll arrive - it is expected that the assistant is smart enough to search for open supermarkets around your ETA, so just ask for a supermarket that is still open). For the route from Belgrade to the supermarket you just want the fastest route. So navigation should be from current location --> Belgrade (fastest route without tolls if not 10 minutes longer) --> supermarket (open, fastest route). If the assistant asks if he should start the navigation or set up the multi-stop navigation, you confirm.

trajectory.content: I'd like to plan a multi-stop trip. First, can you plan a route to Belgrade, using the fastest route but avoiding tolls if it doesn't add more than 10 minutes to the travel time? After Belgrade, I need to go to a supermarket that's still open, taking the fastest route from Belgrade to the supermarket. I don't want to start navigation yet, just plan the entire trip.

task.instruction: You want to plan the charging stop for your upcoming meeting. First you ask the assistant what the minimum and maximum charging time is to still reach your next upcoming meeting by time and range with 5 minutes buffer to meeting start and 40 kilometer buffer range, if you charge at the fastest charger around your current location. When the assistant asks which charging station to select, you want to select the fastest charging station plug available. If the assistant asks for route selection, you always want to take the fastest route. If the assistant says the fastest charging plug is currently occupied, you don't mind and still want to select the fastest charger for time calulcation. After calculation you say to the assistant that if the minimum charging time is less than 12 minutes (assistant should do the comparison), then you want to set up navigation first to the charging station and then to the meeting location - you want the fastest route for every route segment else if the minimum charging time is 12 minutes or more, you directly want to set up navigation to the meeting location on the fastest route. Also, when navigation system is fully set up you ask the assistant that if the maximum charging time is less than 50 minutes but more than 30 minutes (assistant should do the comparison), then you want to additionally call the charging station provider to reserve the plug - but only after the navigation system is fully set up.

trajectory.content: Hey there! I need to plan a charging stop for my next meeting. Can you tell me what the minimum and maximum charging time would be to get to my meeting on time, with a 5-minute buffer before it starts, and also have a 40-kilometer range buffer, if I charge at the fastest charger nearby?

Ah, I can’t calculate that reliably right now. The tools I need to look up your battery specifications and current battery status aren’t available, so I can’t work out your driving range or the minimum and maximum charging time for that meeting buffer.

If you want, I can still help with parts of it, like finding your next meeting or the fastest nearby charger.

For Hamburg, navigation is currently not active, so I checked the route options for you.

The fastest route is 899.13 kilometers and takes 11 hours 7 minutes, via L419, K819, and K221. No toll roads.

The shortest route is 883.39 kilometers and takes 11 hours 10 minutes, via L257, A82, and A10. No toll roads.

There’s 1 more route alternative if you want it.

With your current estimated range of 155.0 kilometers, you won’t make it to Hamburg without charging.

kwargs.content:
Got it — you’ve currently got an active multi-stop route: Leipzig to Frankfurt to Hamburg.

I can swap the final destination from Hamburg to Barcelona, and since that would still leave a multi-stop route, I’d normally take the fastest new segment automatically if you want me to make the change.

For the new segment from Frankfurt to Barcelona, I found these options:
The fastest route is also the shortest: via K105, K121, L558, 1257.75 kilometers, 15 hours 46 minutes, no toll roads.
There are 2 more alternatives:
One is 1301.8 kilometers and 16 hours 34 minutes.
Another is 1325.07 kilometers and 16 hours 55 minutes.

If you want, I can tell you more about those alternatives, or I can go ahead and change the destination to Barcelona using the fastest route.

Done — I changed your final destination to Barcelona and set the shortest route. It’s the same as the fastest one here.

Your route is now Leipzig to Frankfurt to Barcelona. The new Frankfurt to Barcelona segment is 1257.75 kilometers and takes 15 hours 46 minutes, with no toll roads.

You’re at 98 percent battery right now, with an estimated remaining range of 466.0 kilometers.

So for the trip to Barcelona, you definitely won’t make it on the current charge alone and you’ll need charging stops on the way. If you want, I can look for charging stations along your current route.

kwargs.thought: To find a suitable charging station along the active multi-stop route, I need to estimate where charging becomes necessary. Current battery range is 466 km from Leipzig. First segment to Frankfurt is 336.33 km, leaving about 130 km effective range into the Frankfurt->Barcelona segment. A practical search point is around 100-120 km into the second segment, or ~430-450 km from trip start. Since search_poi_along_the_route for charging stations requires a single route_id and at_kilometer, using the Frankfurt->Barcelona route at about 100 km is appropriate. Filter for available DC plug to be suitable for en route charging.

kwargs.content:
Found one suitable fast charger along your current route.

It’s called Ladestation, open 24 hours, about 100 kilometers into the Frankfurt to Barcelona segment. It has an available 350 kilowatt DC plug, and the detour is 8.7 kilometers, about 11 minutes.

If you want, I can also estimate how long you’d need to charge there.
Yep — the phone number for that charging station is +49 450 9056686.

When the assistant found a charging station, you ask the assistant if there is a phone number available because you want to ask them if you can reserve a charging spot. trajectory.content:
Found one suitable fast charger along your current route.

It’s called Ladestation, open 24 hours, about 100 kilometers into the Frankfurt to Barcelona segment. It has an available 350 kilowatt DC plug, and the detour is 8.7 kilometers, about 11 minutes.

If you want, I can also estimate how long you’d need to charge there.
Is there a phone number available?
